content:
Member-only story

17 min read
3 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you’ve ever pasted @Component into an Angular class, written a @Get('/users') in a NestJS controller, or wondered why your TypeScript code looks like Java annotations, you've met decorators. They're one of the more misunderstood features in TypeScript, and one people argue about.
This is the guide I wish existed when I first tripped over @sealed in the TypeScript handbook and had no idea what it did. By the end of it, you'll understand:

• What a decorator actually is (the precise mental model)

• Why there are two completely different decorator APIs in TypeScript right now, and which one you should use

• All five decorator types, with runnable code

• The decorator factory pattern, composition rules, and execution order

• 10 real-world backend patterns (logging, caching, validation, DI, routing, ORM)

• 7 frontend patterns (analytics, persistence, autosave, observable, hotkeys, confirm, component naming)

• The pitfalls that catch even experienced engineers
Also read JavaScript ES2026: Temporal API, Decorators and New Methods -A Complete Guide
1. The 30-Second Mental Model
